channel.title: Tokyo Police Hospital
channel.description: Recent content on Tokyo Police Hospital

item.title: Admission & Discharge
item.description:
Admission & Discharge Support Center

Our Admission & Discharge Support Center coordinates your hospital stay from the moment admission is scheduled until you are safely discharged and connected to appropriate community care.

Phone: 03-5343-5611 (main line) → ask for Admission & Discharge Support Center

Admission Process

Step 1: Outpatient Consultation

Your attending physician will determine whether hospitalization is necessary and schedule your admission date.

Step 2: Admission Booking

The Admission & Discharge Support Center will:

item.title: Director's Message
item.description:
Director’s Message

Shunji Hasegawa, MD, PhD Director, Tokyo Police Hospital

On behalf of everyone at Tokyo Police Hospital, I would like to extend a warm welcome to all our patients, visitors, and partners.

Tokyo Police Hospital was founded in 1929 (Showa Year 4) in Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo, as a dedicated medical institution to support officers of the Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department. In 2008, we relocated to our current location in Nakano-ku, a modern, purpose-built facility designed to meet the medical needs of the 21st century.

item.title: First Visit (New Patients)
item.description:
Before Your First Visit

If you are visiting Tokyo Police Hospital for the first time, please review the information below carefully before your appointment.

Opening Hours

Time
Main Entrance Opens 7:45
Reception Hours (Morning) 8:00 – 11:30
Reception Hours (Afternoon) 12:30 – 16:00

Required Documents

Please bring the following items to the reception desk on your first visit:

  1. Health Insurance Card (or MyNumber Card / Individual Number Card for online insurance verification)
  2. Referral Letter from your regular physician or clinic (strongly recommended)
  3. Medical history summary (if available)
  4. List of current medications (prescription notebooks, medication lists, or actual medications)
  5. Any relevant previous test results (X-rays, blood test results, etc.)
  6. Emergency contact information

Initial Consultation Surcharge

In accordance with national healthcare guidelines, patients who visit without a referral letter from another medical institution are required to pay an initial consultation surcharge of ¥7,700 (tax included) in addition to normal medical fees.

item.title: General Information & Reception
item.description:
General Information Desk

The General Information Desk is located in the main lobby on the 1st Floor, near the main entrance.

Our friendly staff are available to assist with:

  • Directing you to the correct department or clinic
  • Providing information about hospital services
  • Lending wheelchairs and strollers
  • Answering general questions about the hospital

Wheelchair & Stroller Loan

The following mobility aids are available free of charge from the Information Desk:

Item Quantity Available
Wheelchairs 12
Strollers (for young children) 2

Please return items to the Information Desk after use.

item.title: Hospital Overview & History
item.description:
Hospital Overview

Item Details
Hospital Name Tokyo Police Hospital (東京警察病院)
Operator General Foundation Jikkei-kai (一般財団法人 仁恵会)
Director Shunji Hasegawa, MD, PhD
Founded March 18, 1929
Address 4-22-1 Nakano, Nakano-ku, Tokyo 164-8541
Phone 03-5343-5611
Fax 03-5343-5612
Licensed Beds 415 (general ward)
Departments 30 clinical departments
Specialized Centers 10
Building 9 floors above ground, 2 basement floors, rooftop helipad
Designated Hospital Types DPC hospital, Emergency hospital, Cancer designated hospital, etc.

Building Information
Above Ground 9 floors
Below Ground 2 floors
Rooftop Heliport (for helicopter emergency transport)
Year Built / Relocated 2008 (current facility)

Historical Timeline

Year Event
1920 Planning began for a dedicated hospital for the Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department
1929 Tokyo Police Hospital officially founded (March 18) in Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo
1945 Hospital survived World War II; continues operations
1960s–70s Expansion of clinical departments and nursing staff
1980s Establishment of specialized centers; adoption of new diagnostic technologies
1990s Introduction of advanced imaging equipment (MRI, CT); expanded emergency services
2000 Began transition planning for new facility
2008 Relocation to current site in Nakano-ku; new 9-story building opened
2010 Received accreditation from the Japan Council for Quality Health Care (JCQHC)
2015 Introduction of da Vinci robotic surgical system
2018 Designated as Cancer Designated Hospital
2020 Establishment of COVID-19 response protocols; continues to serve community through pandemic
2023 Continued quality improvement; updated DPC classification; expansion of robotic surgery program

Hospital Designation & Certifications

Tokyo Police Hospital holds numerous official designations:

item.title: Medical Documents & Certificates
item.description:
Medical Documents & Certificates

We provide a range of official medical documents for patients, including medical certificates, diagnosis reports, summaries, and insurance-related forms.

Document Counter
Location Counter ⑦, 1st Floor
Phone 03-5343-5670
Hours Monday – Saturday, 8:00 – 17:00

Types of Documents Available

Document Type Notes
Medical Certificate (Shindan-sho) General health/diagnosis certificate
Diagnosis Summary Summary of your condition and treatment
Discharge Summary Issued at or after discharge
Certificate of Surgery Record of surgical procedure
Insurance Claim Form For private insurance companies
Disability Certificate Requires physician review
Return-to-Work Certificate Confirmation of fitness to return to work
School/Work Absence Certificate Confirmation of illness for absence

Application Procedure

  1. Visit Counter ⑦ on the 1st Floor during business hours, or request by mail.
  2. Complete the Application Form available at the counter (or downloadable from the hospital’s Japanese website).
  3. Present your:
    • Patient registration card
    • Photo ID (if collecting on behalf of patient, bring authorization letter and your own ID)
  4. Pay the applicable fee.
  5. Wait approximately 2 weeks for most documents to be prepared.
  6. You will be notified when your document is ready for collection.

Processing Time

Standard processing time is approximately 2 weeks from the date of application.

item.title: Repeat Visit (Returning Patients)
item.description:
Repeat Visit Information

For patients who have previously been seen at Tokyo Police Hospital, please follow the procedures below when scheduling and attending your follow-up visits.

Booking a Repeat Appointment

Repeat Appointment Center
Phone 03-5343-5620 / 03-5343-5621
Available Hours Monday – Saturday, 8:15 – 17:00

You can schedule, change, or cancel appointments by telephone. Please have your patient ID number ready when calling.

Online Appointment Booking

Repeat appointments may also be made online via the hospital’s patient portal. Please ask reception for details.

item.title: Room Types & Fees
item.description:
Room Types and Fees

Tokyo Police Hospital offers a range of room types to suit different needs and preferences. Rooms covered by standard health insurance have no extra room charge. Private rooms carry an additional daily fee, which is not covered by health insurance and must be paid out-of-pocket.

Room Fee Table

Room Type Daily Fee (¥, tax incl.) Size (㎡) Number of Rooms Facilities
4-bed Room Free 32 ㎡ (8㎡/bed) 64 rooms Standard
Private Room A ¥20,900 17 ㎡ 24 rooms Shower & Toilet
Private Room B ¥19,800 18 ㎡ 2 rooms Shower & Toilet
Private Room C ¥18,150 16 ㎡ 55 rooms Toilet only
Private Room D ¥14,630 15 ㎡ 45 rooms No shower/toilet
Special Suite (Standard) ¥37,400 29 ㎡ — Shower & Toilet
Special Suite (Deluxe) ¥52,800 43 ㎡ — Shower & Toilet

All fees are per night and include tax. Room fees are not covered by public health insurance and are charged in addition to your medical expenses.

item.title: Daily Life During Hospitalization
item.description:
Daily Routine

During your stay, each day will generally follow this schedule:

Time Activity
7:45 Breakfast served
12:00 Lunch served
18:00 Dinner served
Morning Doctor’s rounds
Throughout the day Nursing care, therapy sessions, tests
21:00 Lights out (please rest quietly after this time)
22:00 Ward quieted; visitor departures required

Meals

Hospital meals are planned by our registered dietitians and tailored to your medical condition and dietary needs. Meals are served three times a day. If you have food allergies, religious dietary requirements, or other concerns, please inform the dietitian or nursing staff.

item.title: Mission & Philosophy
item.description:
Hospital Mission

“Pursuing excellence in medical quality and patient satisfaction”

At Tokyo Police Hospital, we are committed to providing the highest standard of medical care while placing the well-being, dignity, and satisfaction of every patient at the center of everything we do.

Five Basic Policies

We guide all our activities by the following five principles:

1. Patient-Centered Care

We respect the rights, autonomy, and individual needs of every patient. All medical decisions are made collaboratively, with patients fully informed and actively involved in their care.

item.title: Referral Letter
item.description:
Referral Letters (Shōkaijō)

A referral letter (shōkaijō, 紹介状) is a document issued by your regular physician, clinic, or another hospital that introduces you to a specialist at Tokyo Police Hospital. We strongly encourage all new patients to obtain a referral letter before visiting.

Benefits of Bringing a Referral Letter

  • No initial consultation surcharge: Patients with a referral letter are exempt from the ¥7,700 surcharge.
  • Faster and more efficient care: The referring physician provides relevant medical history, test results, and diagnosis notes, allowing our specialists to assess your condition more quickly.
  • Continuity of care: After your treatment at our hospital, a report will be sent back to your referring physician, ensuring coordinated care.

How to Obtain a Referral Letter

  1. Visit your regular clinic or primary care physician.
  2. Explain that you wish to be referred to Tokyo Police Hospital and the specialty you need.
  3. Your doctor will prepare a referral letter for you.
  4. Bring the sealed referral letter to our reception on the day of your visit.

Medical Liaison Office

For referring physicians and healthcare institutions wishing to send patients to Tokyo Police Hospital:

item.title: Second Opinion Service
item.description:
Second Opinion Service

If you have received a diagnosis or treatment recommendation from another hospital and wish to have it reviewed by one of our specialists, Tokyo Police Hospital offers a Second Opinion Consultation Service.

A second opinion can help you:

  • Confirm or clarify your diagnosis
  • Understand your treatment options more fully
  • Gain confidence in your care plan
  • Explore alternative approaches

Key Requirements

  • A referral letter from your current physician is required. This confirms your diagnosis, current treatment plan, and the specific question(s) you want addressed.
  • All consultations are by appointment only.
  • Second opinion consultations are not covered by public health insurance and must be paid in full by the patient.

Fees

Consultation Duration Fee
Up to 30 minutes ¥15,000 (tax included)
30 minutes – 60 minutes ¥30,000 (tax included)

Fees are paid in advance at the time of booking confirmation. Additional charges may apply if special tests or diagnostic review are required.

item.title: Nutrition Counseling
item.description:
Nutrition Counseling & Guidance

Our team of registered dietitians provides personalized nutrition counseling and education for patients with a variety of health conditions. Good nutrition is a fundamental component of disease prevention, treatment, and recovery.

Individual Nutrition Counseling

Individual consultations are available for patients whose conditions require specific dietary management, including:

  • Diabetes mellitus – carbohydrate management, meal planning, blood glucose control
  • Kidney disease (CKD) – protein, potassium, phosphorus, and fluid management
  • Hypertension – sodium restriction, DASH diet
  • Dyslipidemia (high cholesterol) – fat intake, heart-healthy diet
  • Obesity – calorie management, behavioral change support
  • Cancer and cancer treatment – nutritional support during chemotherapy/radiation
  • Post-surgical nutrition – support for recovery and dietary progression
  • Eating disorders – supportive nutritional counseling
  • Allergies and special diets – food allergy management

How to Arrange

Individual counseling is arranged through your attending physician or the outpatient clinic. Please ask your doctor for a dietitian referral.

item.title: Patient Rights & Responsibilities
item.description:
Patient Rights

Tokyo Police Hospital recognizes and upholds the following rights for all patients:

1. Right to Appropriate Medical Care

Every patient has the right to receive appropriate, safe, and high-quality medical care without discrimination based on gender, age, nationality, religion, disability, or any other characteristic.

2. Right to Information

Patients have the right to receive clear and understandable information about:

  • Their diagnosis and medical condition
  • Proposed treatments and procedures
  • Expected outcomes, benefits, and risks
  • Alternative treatment options
  • The names and qualifications of the healthcare professionals involved in their care

3. Right to Informed Consent

Patients have the right to make informed decisions about their care. No procedure or treatment will be carried out without the patient’s consent, except in medical emergencies where the patient cannot give consent.

item.title: Referral-Focused Medical Institution
item.description:
What Is a Referral-Focused Medical Institution?

Tokyo Police Hospital has been designated as a Referral-Focused Medical Institution (Shōkai Jushū Jūten Iryō Kikan, 紹介受診重点医療機関) under Japan’s national healthcare framework.

This designation recognizes hospitals that primarily provide advanced and specialized medical care for complex cases requiring hospital-level expertise, while encouraging patients with stable or routine conditions to consult with local clinics and general practitioners.

Why This Designation Matters

Japan’s healthcare system is structured so that:

item.title: Visiting Rules
item.description:
Visiting Hours
Visiting Hours 15:00 – 19:00 (daily, including weekends and holidays)
Maximum Visit Duration 1 hour per visit
Maximum Number of Visitors Up to 3 visitors at one time
Minimum Age for Visitors 12 years old and above

We appreciate your understanding and cooperation in following these rules to protect the health and recovery of all patients.

General Rules for Visitors

  1. Masks are required at all times within the hospital building.
  2. Hand hygiene – please use the hand sanitizer provided at the ward entrance before and after visiting.
  3. No food or beverages may be brought into patient rooms unless specifically approved by the attending physician or dietitian.
  4. Please speak quietly and be mindful of other patients resting nearby.
  5. Do not enter other patients’ rooms.
  6. Photography and video recording of other patients or staff is strictly prohibited.

Restrictions on Cut Flowers

To protect patients with compromised immune systems, the following restrictions apply:

item.title: Get Well Email Service
item.description:
Get Well Email Service

Can’t visit in person? Our Get Well Email Service allows family, friends, and colleagues to send warm messages and encouragement to patients admitted at Tokyo Police Hospital.

Messages are received by the hospital, printed, and delivered directly to the patient’s bedside.

How to Use the Service

  1. Compose your message via the dedicated email service form or address.
  2. Include the following information in your message:
    • Patient’s full name
    • Ward name and room number (if known)
    • Your name and relationship to the patient
    • Your message
  3. Send your email to the address provided by hospital staff or on the hospital’s Japanese website.
  4. Hospital staff will print and deliver your message to the patient, typically within 1–2 business days.

Guidelines for Messages

  • Please write in Japanese or English.
  • Keep messages appropriate and positive — this service is meant to encourage patients.
  • Do not include sensitive personal or financial information.
  • The hospital reserves the right to withhold messages that are deemed inappropriate or that may affect the patient’s recovery.

Patient Privacy

The hospital will handle all messages in accordance with our privacy policy. Messages will only be delivered to the named patient. We will not share personal information with third parties.

item.title: Informed Consent Policy
item.description:
Informed Consent at Tokyo Police Hospital

Informed consent is a cornerstone of ethical medical practice and patient-centered care. At Tokyo Police Hospital, we are committed to ensuring that every patient has the information they need to make meaningful, informed decisions about their medical care.

What Is Informed Consent?

Informed consent is the process by which a healthcare provider:

  1. Explains a proposed treatment, procedure, or test to the patient
  2. Discusses the expected benefits and potential risks
  3. Presents alternative options
  4. Answers the patient’s questions
  5. Obtains the patient’s voluntary agreement before proceeding

Consent must be given freely and without coercion, and the patient must have sufficient mental capacity to understand the information provided.

item.title: Medical Social Work
item.description:
Medical Welfare Consultation Office (Medical Social Work)

Our Medical Social Workers (MSWs) are available to provide free, confidential support to patients and their families on a wide range of non-medical concerns that arise during illness and hospitalization.

Hours & Contact
Hours Monday – Saturday, 8:30 – 17:00
Location 1st Floor (ask at the Information Desk or nursing station)
Phone 03-5343-5611 (main) → ask for Medical Welfare Consultation Office

All consultations are free of charge and strictly confidential.

item.title: Medication
item.description:
Medication at Tokyo Police Hospital

Our goal is to ensure that every patient receives safe, effective, and appropriate medication. Please read the following information carefully.

Hospital Pharmacy

The hospital pharmacy is located on the 1st Floor. After your consultation, your physician will issue an electronic prescription, which can be filled at:

  • Hospital Pharmacy (1F) – for immediate pickup on the same day
  • Any community pharmacy of your choice (outside prescription)

We recommend using a designated “Family Pharmacy” (Kari Yakkyoku) near your home for continuity of medication management.

item.title: DPC Payment System
item.description:
What Is the DPC Payment System?

Tokyo Police Hospital uses the Diagnosis Procedure Combination / Per-Diem Payment System (DPC/PDPS) to calculate inpatient medical fees. This system is applied at designated hospitals throughout Japan.

How DPC Works

Under the DPC system, your daily hospitalization cost is calculated using a standardized rate based on:

  1. Your primary diagnosis (classified using the DPC code system)
  2. Your treatment procedures (surgery, specific therapies, etc.)
  3. The length of your stay

Rather than billing individually for each examination, injection, or procedure, DPC consolidates most routine inpatient costs into a single daily package rate. This makes the total cost more predictable and often more affordable than fee-for-service billing.

item.title: Medical Records Disclosure
item.description:
Medical Records Disclosure

In accordance with Japanese law and our hospital’s patient rights policy, patients have the right to access and obtain copies of their own medical records. We handle all requests in compliance with the Act on the Protection of Personal Information.

Application Procedure

  1. Submit a completed Medical Records Disclosure Application Form (available at the Document Counter ⑦, 1F, or on the hospital’s Japanese website).
  2. Present valid photo ID (passport, driver’s license, or My Number card with photo).
  3. If applying on behalf of a patient:
    • Written authorization from the patient
    • Applicant’s own photo ID
    • Relationship documentation if applicable (family register, etc.)
  4. Pay the applicable fees.
  5. Records will be prepared and made available within approximately 2–4 weeks of application.

Fees

Item Fee
Application handling fee ¥3,300 per request (tax included)
Paper medical record copy ¥33 per page (tax included)
Electronic medical record copy ¥22 per page (tax included)
Diagnostic image DVD (per disc) ¥1,100 per disc (tax included)

All fees must be paid in advance. Fees are non-refundable once processing has begun.

item.title: Partnership Program
item.description:
Partnership Program

Tokyo Police Hospital actively fosters partnerships with local clinics, community hospitals, and healthcare providers throughout the Tokyo area. Our partnership program is designed to ensure seamless, continuous care for patients across all levels of the healthcare system.

Why Partnerships Matter

Modern healthcare is most effective when patients can move smoothly between different levels of care:

  • Primary Care (Local Clinics): Routine health management, preventive care, chronic disease monitoring
  • Secondary Care (General Hospitals): Diagnosis and treatment of conditions requiring specialist expertise
  • Tertiary Care (Specialized Centers): Complex surgery, advanced technology, intensive care

Tokyo Police Hospital occupies the secondary and tertiary care levels. Our partnerships ensure that patients have access to appropriate primary care and that transitions between care levels are well-coordinated.

item.title: Facilities & Amenities
item.description:
Hospital Facilities & Amenities

Tokyo Police Hospital’s modern facility offers a range of amenities to support patients, families, and visitors during their time at the hospital.

Convenience Store

Natural Lawson (Located on the 1st Floor)
Hours 6:00 – 21:00 (daily)
ATM Available

Natural Lawson offers a selection of fresh foods, snacks, beverages, personal care items, and daily necessities. An ATM is available for cash withdrawals (fees may apply for non-Lawson bank cards).

item.title: Multidisciplinary Support Teams
item.description:
Multidisciplinary Support Teams

Tokyo Police Hospital operates a comprehensive network of multidisciplinary support teams that work across all departments to ensure the highest standards of patient safety, care quality, and clinical outcomes.

Each team brings together specialists from different disciplines — physicians, nurses, pharmacists, dietitians, therapists, and others — to provide expert support to clinical teams and patients throughout the hospital.

ICT — Infection Control Team

The Infection Control Team (ICT) monitors, prevents, and manages hospital-acquired infections throughout the facility.

item.title: Accreditations & Certifications
item.description:
Official Designations (Government & Public Body)

Tokyo Police Hospital holds the following official designations from national and metropolitan government bodies:

Designation Issuing Body
Emergency Hospital (Level 2) Tokyo Metropolitan Government
Cancer Designated Hospital Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare / Tokyo
DPC Standard Hospital Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare
Referral-Focused Medical Institution Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare
Disaster Medical Assistance Team (DMAT) Base Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare
Stroke Center Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare
Clinical Training Hospital Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare
Nursing Training Hospital Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare
Medical Intern Training Facility Japan Medical Association

Medical Society Accreditations

The following departments and programs hold accreditation from their respective Japanese medical societies:

item.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
item.description:
Frequently Asked Questions

Outpatient & First Visit

Q: Do I need a referral letter to visit Tokyo Police Hospital? A: You do not need a referral letter to visit our outpatient clinic. However, patients visiting without a referral letter will be charged an additional initial consultation surcharge of ¥7,700 (tax included). We strongly encourage obtaining a referral letter from your regular physician to avoid this charge and to facilitate more efficient care.

item.title: Advanced Medical Equipment
item.description:
Advanced Medical Equipment

Tokyo Police Hospital is equipped with state-of-the-art diagnostic and therapeutic technology, enabling our specialists to deliver accurate diagnoses and effective treatments with minimal patient discomfort.

da Vinci Xi Robotic Surgical System

The da Vinci Xi is the latest generation of robotic-assisted surgical technology. It allows our surgeons to perform complex minimally invasive procedures with:

  • Greater precision and 3D visualization
  • Smaller incisions, less blood loss
  • Faster recovery and shorter hospital stays

Robotic surgery is available for urological, gastrointestinal, thoracic, and gynecological procedures.

item.title: Patient Safety
item.description:
Patient Safety at Tokyo Police Hospital

Patient safety is the highest priority at Tokyo Police Hospital. We maintain a comprehensive, hospital-wide safety management program to prevent errors, identify risks, and continuously improve the quality of care we deliver.

Our Safety Management System

Safety Management Committee

The Patient Safety Management Committee oversees all aspects of safety across the hospital. This committee includes the hospital director, department heads, nursing leadership, pharmacists, and other key staff.

item.title: Mobile Phone Policy
item.description:
Mobile Phone & Electronic Device Policy

To protect the privacy of all patients and to prevent interference with medical equipment, Tokyo Police Hospital has established the following rules for the use of mobile phones, smartphones, tablets, and laptops.

Permitted Areas

Mobile phone use is permitted in the following areas:

  • Lobby and corridor areas on the 1st Floor
  • Designated patient lounge areas on ward floors
  • Convenience store and café area (Natural Lawson, Tully’s Coffee)
  • Outdoor areas on hospital grounds (excluding smoking restrictions)

Prohibited Areas

Mobile phone use is strictly prohibited in the following areas:

item.title: Policy on Refusal of Blood Transfusion
item.description:
Policy on Patient Refusal of Blood Transfusion

Tokyo Police Hospital respects every patient’s right to make informed decisions about their medical care, including the right to refuse specific treatments on religious, personal, or ethical grounds.

Our Approach

When a patient requests that blood transfusion be withheld or refused, our hospital follows the guidelines established by the relevant Japanese medical societies and the Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare.

Informed Discussion

If you or a family member holds religious or personal beliefs against blood transfusion (for example, as a Jehovah’s Witness), please inform the medical team as early as possible — ideally before admission or surgical procedures.

item.title: Hospital Evaluation (JCQHC)
item.description:
Japan Council for Quality Health Care (JCQHC) Accreditation

Tokyo Police Hospital has received accreditation from the Japan Council for Quality Health Care (公益財団法人 日本医療機能評価機構, JCQHC), Japan’s leading independent hospital accreditation body.

What Is JCQHC Accreditation?

The JCQHC conducts comprehensive, independent assessments of hospitals across Japan to evaluate the quality and safety of medical care. The assessment process includes:

  • On-site review by a team of trained surveyors
  • Evaluation against standardized quality criteria across all hospital functions
  • Assessment of patient safety systems, infection control, patient rights, medical records management, and organizational governance
  • Feedback and recommendations for continuous improvement

Hospitals that meet the required standards are granted accreditation, which must be renewed periodically through re-evaluation.

item.title: Suggestions & Feedback
item.description:
Suggestions & Feedback

Tokyo Police Hospital values every comment, suggestion, and concern from our patients, families, and visitors. Your feedback is essential for helping us improve our services and the quality of care we provide.

Suggestion Boxes

Suggestion boxes are located at the following locations throughout the hospital:

  • Main lobby (1st Floor, near Information Desk)
  • Outpatient waiting areas (2F, 3F)
  • Ward entrances (5F–9F)

Submission forms and pens are available next to each box. You may submit anonymously, or include your contact information if you would like a response.

item.title: Assistance Dogs Policy
item.description:
Assistance Dogs at Tokyo Police Hospital

In accordance with the Act for Assistance Dog Users (Houshin Jiritsushien Hou), Tokyo Police Hospital welcomes and accommodates users of all certified assistance dogs, including:

  • Guide Dogs (Mōdōken) — for persons with visual impairments
  • Service Dogs / Mobility Assistance Dogs (Kaitejoken) — for persons with physical disabilities
  • Hearing Dogs (Hochōken) — for persons who are deaf or hard of hearing

Certified assistance dogs are permitted in all areas of the hospital that their user needs to access, including outpatient areas, wards, and support areas.

item.title: Hospital Statistics
item.description:
Hospital Performance Indicators

In the interest of transparency, Tokyo Police Hospital publishes key performance indicators annually. These statistics provide insight into the scope and quality of care we deliver.

The figures below are based on the most recently available annual data. Updated statistics are published each year on the hospital’s Japanese website.

Inpatient Statistics

Indicator Value
Licensed Beds 415 (general ward)
Average Bed Occupancy Rate Approx. 80–85%
Average Length of Stay Approx. 9–11 days
Annual Inpatient Admissions Approx. 10,000–12,000
Nurse-to-Patient Ratio (Acute Ward) 1:7 (Type 1)

Outpatient Statistics

Indicator Value
Annual Outpatient Visits Approx. 200,000+
Daily Outpatient Volume Approx. 600–700 patients
Departments with Outpatient Clinics 30

Emergency Department

Indicator Value
Annual Emergency Patients 5,000+
Emergency Availability 24 hours / 365 days

Surgical Statistics

Indicator Value
Annual Surgical Cases Approx. 3,000–4,000
Of which, Laparoscopic / Minimally Invasive Approx. 40–50%
Robotic-Assisted Surgery Cases Increasing year-on-year

Cancer Care

Indicator Value
Annual New Cancer Diagnoses Approx. 1,000+
Multidisciplinary Cancer Board (MDT) Meetings Weekly
Radiation Therapy Cases Approx. 300–400/year

Quality Indicators

Indicator Value
Unplanned Readmission Rate (30 days) Below national average
Surgical Site Infection Rate Below national benchmark
Patient Satisfaction Rate Approx. 85–90% (Good/Very Good)

Staffing

Staff Category Number (Approx.)
Physicians (full-time) 150+
Nurses 600+
Pharmacists 30+
Physical / Occupational / Speech Therapists 50+
Registered Dietitians 15+
Medical Social Workers 10+
Total Staff 1,200+

Note on Data

Detailed annual statistics and quality reports are published in accordance with Japanese Ministry of Health requirements. For the most current figures, please consult the Japanese hospital website or contact the hospital administration office.

item.title: Clinical Trials & Research
item.description:
Clinical Trials & Medical Research

Tokyo Police Hospital is committed to advancing medical knowledge and improving patient care through rigorous clinical research. We participate in clinical trials and observational studies in collaboration with academic institutions, research organizations, and pharmaceutical companies.

What Are Clinical Trials?

Clinical trials are research studies that test new treatments, medications, diagnostic procedures, or care approaches in human participants. They are conducted in accordance with strict ethical guidelines and regulatory requirements to ensure patient safety and scientific validity.

item.title: Women's Empowerment Initiative
item.description:
Women’s Empowerment Action Plan

Tokyo Police Hospital has formulated an Action Plan for the Promotion of Women’s Empowerment in accordance with the Act on Promotion of Women’s Participation and Advancement in the Workplace (女性活躍推進法).

Plan Period

April 1, 2021 – March 31, 2026 (5 years)

Current Status (Baseline Data)

Indicator Current Value
Female staff as proportion of total workforce 69.8%
Female managers as proportion of all managers 34.9%
Average tenure (female employees) Reference year baseline

Healthcare is a field where women represent the majority of the workforce. At Tokyo Police Hospital, women make up nearly 70% of all employees. We are committed to ensuring that this reflects not only numbers, but also leadership, career development, and equal opportunity at every level.

item.title: Next-Generation Support Plan
item.description:
Next-Generation Support Action Plan

Tokyo Police Hospital has formulated an Action Plan for the Promotion of Measures to Support the Development of the Next Generation in accordance with the Act on Advancement of Measures to Support Raising Next-Generation Children (次世代育成支援対策推進法).

Plan Period

April 1, 2025 – March 31, 2030 (5 years)

Background

Creating a workplace that supports employees in balancing their professional responsibilities with family life — including raising children and caring for elderly relatives — is essential for the long-term sustainability of our workforce and the quality of care we provide to patients.

item.title: Information Disclosure
item.description:
Information Disclosure

In accordance with Japanese healthcare regulations, Tokyo Police Hospital discloses the following information to patients and the public.

DPC Hospital Classification

Tokyo Police Hospital is a designated DPC (Diagnosis Procedure Combination) Hospital, classified under the Standard DPC Group.

Item Value
DPC Classification Standard DPC Group
Medical Institution Coefficient 1.5771

For details on how DPC affects your hospitalization costs, see DPC Payment System.

Itemized Billing

Patients have the right to request an itemized bill (meisai) at any time, free of charge. This document provides a detailed breakdown of all charges applied during your visit or hospital stay.

item.title: Access & Directions
item.description:
Access & Directions

Hospital Address

Tokyo Police Hospital (東京警察病院) 4-22-1 Nakano, Nakano-ku, Tokyo 164-8541

  • Phone: 03-5343-5611
  • Fax: 03-5343-5612

By Train

The most convenient way to reach the hospital is by train to Nakano Station.

Line Station Exit Walk
JR Chuo Line Nakano Station North Exit Approx. 10 min walk
Tokyo Metro Tozai Line Nakano Station North Exit Approx. 10 min walk

From Nakano Station North Exit, turn left and head north along the main road. The hospital is a large 9-story building clearly visible from the road.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description:
Privacy Policy

Tokyo Police Hospital (Tōkyō Keisatsu Byōin), operated by the General Foundation Jikkei-kai, is committed to protecting the personal information of all patients, families, staff, and visitors in accordance with the Act on the Protection of Personal Information (Kojin Jōhō Hogo Hō) and all applicable Japanese laws and regulations.

1. Information We Collect

In the course of providing medical care, we collect the following types of personal information:

  • Name, date of birth, gender, and contact details
  • Health insurance information and insurance card numbers
  • Medical history, diagnosis, treatment records, and test results
  • Prescription and medication records
  • Images (X-rays, CT, MRI, photographs)
  • Billing and payment information
  • Information provided voluntarily by patients or their representatives

2. Purpose of Use

Personal information collected by Tokyo Police Hospital is used for the following purposes:
